verificationNote: Risk register items are constructed examples for OT/ICS environments based on real-world incidents and IEC 62443 requirements. Organisations must develop their own risk register based on site-specific assessment. Not endorsed by any standards body.
title: OT/ICS Risk Register
description: 20 OT/ICS-specific risks covering safety, process integrity, remote access, legacy systems, supply chain, IT/OT convergence, and compliance. Each risk includes inherent and residual scoring, existing controls, and treatment plans.

risks.title: Safety Instrumented System (SIS) compromise
risks.description: Attacker gains access to Safety Instrumented System and modifies safety logic to prevent emergency shutdown, enabling a TRITON/TRISIS-style attack. The SIS is the last line of defence against catastrophic process events.
existingControls: SIS physically isolated from DCS/IT network
SIS key switch in Run mode
risks.treatmentPlan: Maintain physical isolation with annual cable trace verification. Deploy SIS-specific intrusion detection. Implement data diode for any required SIS data export. Quarterly SIS program integrity verification.

risks.title: Unauthorised modification of PLC logic
risks.description: Attacker or insider modifies PLC/RTU program logic to alter process behaviour without triggering operator alarms. Can cause equipment damage, product contamination, or unsafe process states.
existingControls: Engineering workstation access restricted to authorised engineers
PLC program backup and checksum register
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy PLC program change detection monitoring. Enforce PLC key switch to Run mode during normal operations. Implement PAM for engineering workstation access. Monthly PLC program integrity audit.

risks.title: IT/OT lateral movement via converged network
risks.description: Attacker compromises IT network and pivots into OT through inadequate segmentation, shared credentials, or IDMZ bypass. Initial compromise is typically via phishing or internet-facing IT service.
existingControls: IDMZ implemented between IT and OT
Separate IT and OT Active Directory domains
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct quarterly IDMZ firewall rule review. Deploy OT network monitoring with IT/OT boundary alerts. Eliminate any remaining shared credentials between IT and OT. Annual penetration test of IT-to-OT boundary.
risks.owner: OT Security Manager / IT Security Manager

risks.title: Historian database manipulation
risks.description: Attacker modifies historian data to conceal malicious process changes, corrupt operational records used for decision-making, or tamper with evidence needed for NACSA s26 incident reporting.
existingControls: Historian write access restricted to SCADA data bus
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement historian anomaly detection for unexpected writes. Deploy write-once historian archiving for compliance data. Restrict historian network access via IDMZ proxy only.

risks.title: Legacy protocol exploitation (Modbus/DNP3)
risks.description: Attacker with OT network access exploits unauthenticated legacy protocols (Modbus TCP, DNP3, OPC Classic) to read process data, modify setpoints, or issue commands to field devices. No credentials required -- only network access.
existingControls: Network segmentation limits access to OT protocols
OT monitoring detects anomalous protocol traffic
Firewall DPI for OT protocol function code filtering
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy OT protocol-aware DPI on all zone boundaries. Implement allowlisting for permitted Modbus/DNP3 function codes. Evaluate migration to authenticated protocols (OPC UA, Modbus/TCP Security) during next upgrade cycle.

risks.title: Remote access abuse to SCADA systems
risks.description: Attacker compromises remote access credentials (VPN, remote desktop, vendor portal) to gain direct access to SCADA/HMI systems. Remote access is the most common initial access vector in OT incidents.
existingControls: Dedicated OT remote access gateway in IDMZ
MFA enforced for all remote access
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce just-in-time access with per-session approval. Deploy behavioural analytics on remote sessions. Quarterly remote access account review. Disable VPN split tunnelling for OT-destined connections.

risks.title: Firmware supply chain attack on OT components
risks.description: Compromised firmware delivered via vendor update channel or supply chain injection. Malicious firmware on PLC, RTU, or network equipment provides persistent attacker access that survives reimaging.
risks.treatmentPlan: Require SBOM from all OT vendors. Verify firmware signatures before deployment. Establish isolated test environment for firmware validation. Monitor vendor security advisories. Implement firmware attestation where supported.

risks.title: Insider threat to process control systems
risks.description: Disgruntled or coerced insider with legitimate OT access modifies process controls, disables safety systems, or exfiltrates sensitive OT data. Insider has knowledge of process and bypass methods.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement user behaviour analytics for OT access patterns. Enforce separation of duties for critical process changes. Quarterly access reviews with HR leavers cross-check. Immediate access revocation procedure on termination.

risks.title: Physical damage through cyber attack
risks.description: Cyber attack causes physical damage to OT equipment by manipulating process parameters beyond safe operating limits (e.g., Stuxnet centrifuge destruction, pipeline overpressure). Damage may be irreversible and require equipment replacement.
existingControls: Process variable limits configured in PLC/DCS
Operator training on abnormal process conditions
risks.treatmentPlan: Verify SIS independence with physical isolation verification. Implement hardcoded process limits in SIS logic (not only DCS). Deploy process anomaly detection on critical process variables. Annual HAZOP review considering cyber-physical scenarios.

risks.title: DCS configuration drift from approved baseline
risks.description: Unauthorised or undocumented changes to DCS configuration cause the system to deviate from the approved secure baseline. Drift can introduce vulnerabilities, disable security controls, or cause process instability.
existingControls: Configuration baseline documents per system type
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy configuration monitoring with automated deviation alerts. Cross-reference deviations with change management records. Any unreconciled deviation treated as security incident. Monthly baseline compliance report.

risks.title: OT network segmentation failure
risks.description: Network segmentation controls fail or are bypassed, allowing unauthorised traffic between zones. Caused by misconfigured firewalls, undocumented network connections, or temporary connections left permanent.
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy continuous network monitoring to detect cross-zone traffic violations. Quarterly firewall rule audit. Annual network segmentation verification test. Implement automated alerting for any new cross-zone connections.

risks.title: Unpatched HMI vulnerability exploitation
risks.description: Attacker exploits known vulnerability in HMI software (Windows-based SCADA clients, web-based HMIs) that has not been patched due to vendor certification delay or uptime constraints.
risks.treatmentPlan: Prioritise HMI patching within vendor-qualified timeframes. Deploy application allowlisting on all HMI workstations. Implement network micro-segmentation for HMI zone. Track vendor advisory response times.

risks.title: Wireless sensor network manipulation
risks.description: Attacker intercepts or injects data on wireless sensor networks (WirelessHART, ISA100.11a, LoRaWAN) used for process monitoring, causing incorrect process data or denial of monitoring capability.
existingControls: Wireless network segregated from control network
Physical security around wireless access points
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce wireless protocol encryption and authentication. Deploy wireless intrusion detection. Validate wireless sensor data against wired redundant sensors for safety-critical measurements.

risks.title: Engineering workstation compromise
risks.description: Attacker compromises engineering workstation (EWS) used to program PLCs, configure DCS, and modify safety systems. EWS has the highest privilege level in OT -- it can reprogram any controller it connects to.
existingControls: EWS access restricted to authorised engineers
EWS not connected to internet or email
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce hardware-based MFA for EWS login. Deploy EDR with OT-aware policies. Restrict EWS network connectivity to only target controllers during maintenance windows. EWS activity logging with daily review.

risks.title: Vendor remote maintenance abuse
risks.description: OT vendor with remote maintenance access performs unauthorised actions, introduces vulnerable software, or their access is compromised by third-party attacker. Vendor access often has elevated privileges.
existingControls: Session recording for all vendor access
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce just-in-time vendor access with automatic credential expiry. Review all vendor session recordings within 48 hours. Annual vendor security assessment. Contractual requirement for vendor incident notification.

risks.title: Loss of OT visibility and monitoring
risks.description: OT monitoring platform (passive network monitoring, SIEM integration) fails or is disabled, leaving the organisation blind to OT security events. Attacker may deliberately disable monitoring as part of attack chain.
existingControls: SIEM alerting on log source failure
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement monitoring-of-monitoring: alert on sensor heartbeat failure within 15 minutes. Deploy out-of-band monitoring health check. Establish procedure for operations during monitoring outage. Quarterly monitoring coverage audit.

risks.title: Environmental monitoring system failure through cyber attack
risks.description: Cyber attack disables or manipulates environmental monitoring systems (emissions, effluent, radiation), causing unreported environmental damage or false compliance reporting.
existingControls: Environmental monitoring on isolated network segment
risks.treatmentPlan: Isolate environmental monitoring from process control network. Implement integrity checks on environmental data. Manual sampling programme as independent verification. Alert on environmental monitoring system availability loss.

risks.title: OT backup and recovery failure
risks.description: OT system backups are corrupted, incomplete, or untested, resulting in inability to recover from ransomware or destructive attack. Recovery time exceeds defined RTO, causing extended plant outage.
risks.treatmentPlan: Expand restore testing to include PLC and DCS functional restoration (not just file restore). Maintain offline golden image for critical systems. Quarterly restore test for highest-criticality systems. Verify backup scope includes PLC programs, not just server images.

risks.title: Cross-site cascade through shared control systems
risks.description: Shared SCADA or control system architecture across multiple sites allows a compromise at one site to cascade to others. Common in utilities with centralised SCADA and distributed field sites.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement site-level isolation capability (ability to disconnect individual sites from central SCADA). Deploy per-site intrusion detection. Ensure each site can operate in standalone mode during central system compromise. Annual cross-site cascade exercise.

risks.title: IEC 62443 compliance gap leading to NACSA enforcement action
risks.description: Organisation fails to achieve required IEC 62443 security level (SL-T) for NCII-designated assets, resulting in non-compliance finding during NACSA s23 audit. Regulatory consequences include directives, penalties, or increased audit frequency.
risks.treatmentPlan: Complete SL gap remediation per roadmap. Conduct internal pre-audit assessment 3 months before NACSA audit. Prioritise high-impact SRs (SR-5.1, SR-1.1, SR-3.4) for early closure. Maintain evidence portfolio for all implemented controls.
